repositories.brief_summary: A focused week fixing type system regressions, improving ephemeron runtime internals, and adding C compiler warning coverage.
repositories.new_features_summary: New higher-level ephemeron list API added to the C runtime, plus a reserved_header_bits config value exposed to ocamltest.
repositories.new_features:
- **Ephemeron list API** ([#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) added higher-level helper functions to `runtime/caml/weak.h` — `caml_ephe_list_tail`, `caml_ephe_list_cons`, `caml_ephe_list_append`, `caml_ephe_list_pop` and variants — refactoring `major_gc.c` to use these abstractions and reducing duplication.
- **`reserved_header_bits` exposed to ocamltest** ([#14744](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14744)): [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) added `val reserved_header_bits : int` to `ocamltest_config.mli`, allowing tests to query how many bits of a block header are reserved, and fixing `atomic-locs/cmm.ml` when ocamlopt is absent.
- **`-Wformat-signedness` C compiler warning** ([#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14694)): [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) enabled the `-Wformat-signedness` warning when available, fixing discrepancies between `%x`-family format specifiers and signed/unsigned argument types across the runtime.
repositories.activity_summary: Several PRs opened targeting Windows bugs, ephemeron GC correctness, type system improvements, and a large extensible IO channels proposal.
repositories.activity:
- [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) opened [#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) **extensible IO channels**, an ambitious implementation of the [modular IO RFC](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/blob/master/rfcs/modular_io.md) adding a polymorphic channel type alongside the existing native channels.
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened [#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774) **Ephemerons: wait on all domains before asking for another marking round**, continuing the multi-PR ephemeron GC correctness series.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [#14763](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14763) **Lambda: merge nested labelled applications**, addressing a spurious intermediary closure created when partial application of optional-argument functions is composed.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [#14772](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14772) **typechecker debugging: better unscoped printing**, adding a printer for unscoped identifiers using a `U:` prefix to avoid confusion in debug output.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [#14770](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14770) **no more rigid variants mode for unification**, proposing to remove the rigid variants mode added as a class-constraint fix.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769) **testsuite: document a new loop in conjunctive types after Keep_expansion**, adding a regression test for a typechecker loop with heavy use of conjunctive types.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [#14771](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14771) **debugger: breakpoint on tfunctor**, making it possible to set a breakpoint on a module-dependent function.
- [Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) opened [#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764) **Fix socket_poll_add in select_win32.c**, fixing a logic error where `socket_poll_add` returns the dispatched node rather than the updated list head, causing corrupted SELECT operations.
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) opened [#14761](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14761) **configure: fix build error on OpenSUSE**, addressing a conflict between `--with-relative-libdir` and `--libdir` on systems where `libdir` defaults to `${exec_prefix}/lib64`.
- [Anarchos](https://github.com/Sylvain78) opened [#14773](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14773) **[Haiku] Unix socket test + single-thread setlocale + signals**, adapting the testsuite for Haiku OS specifics.
- Merged [#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735) ([Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)): runtime refactor adding higher-level ephemeron list operations.
- Merged [#14768](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14768) ([Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel)): fix for [#14766](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14766) type checker regression in `nondep_type_rec_aux`.
- Merged [#14709](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14709) ([Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)): ocamldoc now uses `Module_name (Library name)` page title format instead of `Library name: Module_name`.
- Merged [#14755](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14755) ([Yang Lin](https://github.com/y1lan)): corrected a broken link to `ocamltest/OCAMLTEST.adoc` in `testsuite/HACKING.adoc`.
- Merged [#14744](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14744) ([Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole)): fixed the `atomic-locs/cmm.ml` testsuite entry when `ocamlopt` is absent.
- Merged [#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14694) ([Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)): `-Wformat-signedness` C compiler warning enabled.
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion on two Windows-specific bugs and a configure regression on OpenSUSE-like systems with non-standard libdir paths.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14767) **Win32: opendir returns EUNKNOWNERR -267** — [Adrián Montesinos González](https://github.com/amongonz) reported that `Sys.readdir` fails on Windows with an unmapped error code; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) confirmed the bug and suggested mapping `ERROR_DIRECTORY` to `ENOTDIR`.
- [#14758](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14758) **[Win32] `Unix.select` raises 'original file handle not found'** — [Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) provided a detailed analysis of the bug, discovered an additional logic error in `socket_poll_add`, and opened [#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764) with a fix; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) encouraged them to submit a patch.
- [#14760](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14760) **OCaml 5.5.0~beta1 configure failure on OpenSUSE** — [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) traced the issue to `libdir=${exec_prefix}/lib64` triggering a false conflict with `--with-relative-libdir`, and opened [#14761](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14761) with a fix.
- [#14765](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14765) **Sys_error exception in Format.flush_standard_formatters** — [Olivier Andrieu](https://github.com/oandrieu) reported that closing stdout before program exit causes an uncaught `Sys_error` from OCaml's format library finalizers.
repositories.trends_summary: Ephemeron GC correctness and Windows compatibility are clear focus areas, with several overlapping PRs from Gabriel Scherer tackling edge cases.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Ephemeron GC overhaul**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has a series of related PRs ([#14722](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14722), [#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732), [#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733), [#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734), [#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735), [#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745), [#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774)) systematically fixing ephemeron marking correctness and refactoring the runtime, following bugs exposed by multicore randomized testing.
- **Windows platform fixes**: Multiple issues ([#14758](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14758), [#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14767)) and PRs ([#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764)) highlight continued effort to improve Win32 compatibility in Unix and I/O subsystems.
- **Type system regression tracking**: The [#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648) well-foundedness check continues to surface edge cases ([#14754](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14754), [#14766](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14766), [#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769)), keeping type system authors active in reactive fixes.
repositories.contributors_summary: Six contributors landed commits this week, with Stefan Muenzel and Florian Angeletti each contributing multiple fixes and new PRs.
repositories.contributors:
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) merged the ephemeron list refactor ([#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)) and opened a follow-up PR on multi-domain ephemeron marking ([#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774)).
- [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) diagnosed and fixed the type checker regression [#14766](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14766) via [#14768](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14768), also adding a `U:` prefix for unscoped ident printing.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) merged the ocamldoc title format fix ([#14709](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14709)) and opened four new PRs on Lambda, typechecker debugging, and type system cleanup.
- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) landed the `-Wformat-signedness` C warning ([#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14694)).
- [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) fixed the testsuite `atomic-locs` script for bytecode-only builds ([#14744](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14744)).
- [Yang Lin](https://github.com/y1lan) corrected a documentation link in the testsuite ([#14755](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14755)).
- [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) opened the large extensible IO channels proposal ([#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775)).
- [Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) reported and submitted a fix for a Windows `select` bug ([#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764)).
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) reported and fixed the OpenSUSE configure issue ([#14761](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14761)).

repositories.brief_summary: New PR to remove chdir usage opened, plus two new issues on Windows case-sensitivity and depexts variable expansion.
repositories.activity_summary: One new PR opened for chdir removal; ongoing work on cmdliner completions, Nushell support, disk space optimizations, and repos-config syntax.
repositories.activity:
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) opened [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) to **remove use of `chdir`** from the codebase, porting work from [#5966](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5966) as requested in [#6600](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6600); this small but targeted change touches 9 files and is expected to also fix the long-standing [#3171](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3171)
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) continues advancing a four-part **cmdliner completion overhaul**: source compatibility with newer cmdliner ([#6854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6854)), vendored cmdliner updated to 2.1.1 ([#6867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6867)), argument definitions updated with completion functions ([#6868](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6868)), and final installation of cmdliner-based completions with new **PowerShell support** ([#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869), closing [#6853](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6853))
- [@jneem](https://github.com/jneem) is working on **Nushell shell support** for `opam env` via JSON output in [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873), continuing [#6330](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6330); discussion this week focused on version-checking challenges with older Nushell releases
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) has two open **disk space reduction** PRs: removing the build directory earlier during package installation ([#6906](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6906), fixing [#5884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5884)) and caching only dev packages in the internal sources directory ([#6440](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6440), fixing [#5448](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5448) and [#4056](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4056)); live tests report max disk+RAM usage dropping from 4-10GB to under 2GB
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) is pursuing a new **`repos-config` file syntax** with labeled fields per repository section in [#6393](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6393), fixing [#6327](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6327) and queued behind [#6417](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6417) and [#6839](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6839)
repositories.discussions_summary: Two new issues raised: Windows case-sensitivity bug in menhir upgrade and depexts variable expansion not working for MSYS2.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Olivier Andrieu](https://github.com/oandrieu) filed [#6909](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6909) describing a **Windows case-sensitivity bug** where upgrading `menhir` on a case-insensitive NTFS filesystem causes `menhirLib` and `menhirSdk` directories to be silently lowercased during installation, breaking subsequent builds with dune unable to locate `menhirSdk`; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) confirmed this is a novel bug and requested additional diagnostics from the `.opam-switch/install/` tracking files
- [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) opened [#6908](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6908) reporting that **`depexts` package name strings are never subject to `%{var}%` interpolation**, causing MSYS2 users to receive literal `%{msys2:package-prefix}%-gcc` strings passed to `pacman` instead of the resolved package name; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) noted this would complicate UX (repeated depext queries per install) and referenced the related [#5836](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5836)
repositories.trends_summary: Development focus is on Windows/portability improvements, shell completion modernization, and disk usage reduction.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Windows portability** is a recurring theme with the new chdir removal PR ([#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)), the NTFS case-sensitivity bug report ([#6909](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6909)), and the MSYS2 depexts interpolation issue ([#6908](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6908)) all surfacing Windows-specific edge cases in the same week
- **Shell completion modernization** is progressing through a coordinated series of PRs ([#6854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6854), [#6867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6867), [#6868](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6868), [#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869)) that replace legacy completion scripts with cmdliner-driven generation and extend support to PowerShell and Nushell ([#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873))
- **Disk and memory efficiency** during package installation is being addressed from two angles simultaneously: early build directory cleanup ([#6906](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6906)) and selective source caching ([#6440](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6440))
repositories.contributors_summary: Nathan Rebours opened one new PR; Kate, Brian Ward, jneem, and R. Boujbel have ongoing PRs with activity this week.
repositories.contributors:

repositories.brief_summary: Index pointer system merged to reduce index file sizes; menhirLib snapshot updated.
repositories.new_features_summary: Merged indexes now point to each other, reducing file sizes; outdated store detection added.
repositories.new_features:
- **Index pointer system** for `ocaml-index`: merged indexes now store pointers to constituent index files rather than embedding their data inline, resulting in smaller index files and fewer write operations. The project index is now defined by all its index files collectively, not only the final aggregation ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
- **`Outdated_store` exception** added to `Granular_marshal` to detect when a referenced index file is missing or has a mismatched ID; the `ocaml-index` CLI now catches this exception and prints a user-friendly message with a hint to run `dune build @ocaml-index` ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
- **`is_on_disk` predicate** added to the `Granular_marshal` API to test whether a link is stored in another index file ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
- **`write` API signature change**: the `~id` labelled parameter is now required to embed a unique index UID in each written index file, enabling cross-index identity checks ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
- **MenhirLib snapshot refreshed** using the new upstream `--dump-menhirLib` flag (available since menhir 20230608), resolving copy-rule failures with recent menhir versions; menhir version reference updated in README ([#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058))
repositories.activity_summary: One open PR proposes LRU-based memory bounding for the indexer; two PRs were merged this week.
repositories.activity:
- **LRU cache for indexer memory** proposed by [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) remains open with active review from [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w); the PR replaces an unbounded `Hashtbl` with an LRU to cap memory use, but currently has merge conflicts and ongoing discussion about `Duplicate` link handling ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050))
- **Index pointer support** merged by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d), reviewed and merged by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos), including regression tests added to `union.t` for the outdated-store scenario ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
- **MenhirLib snapshot update** merged by [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis), updating the vendored parser and type-parser generated files to reflect the current menhir version ([#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058))
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on PR #2050 covers LRU semantics and correct handling of Duplicate links in the index writer.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Active code-review exchange between [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) and [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) in [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050) on how `Duplicate` links should behave inside the new LRU-backed `write` path, including a discussion of existential type constraints that currently require `Obj.magic`
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) noted in [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051) that partial results for occurrence queries would be preferable when some index files are out-of-sync, flagging it as a known limitation for future work
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing investment in ocaml-index scalability: smaller files, bounded memory, and better error diagnostics.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Sustained focus on **ocaml-index scalability**: the pointer-based index format ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) and the proposed LRU cache ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)) both target reducing resource consumption for large projects
- Growing attention to **user-facing error quality** in the index tooling, with actionable CLI hints replacing silent failures when index state is inconsistent ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
repositories.contributors_summary: Tim ats, Thomas Refis, and Ulysse contributed merged work; Charlène Gros and art-w have ongoing review activity.
repositories.contributors:
- [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) authored the index pointer feature, added regression tests, and addressed review feedback across multiple iterations ([#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
- [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) updated the vendored menhirLib snapshot and accompanying README ([#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058))
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed and merged [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051), provided detailed review feedback on exception handling and partial-results behaviour, and fixed the changelog entry post-merge ([b3ee185](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/commit/b3ee1852972560f8ba8dec2430a9211e22ffc7c4))
- [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) is actively developing the LRU indexer cache ([#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)) and responding to detailed review feedback
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) provided extensive review on [#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050) including guidance on type-safe alternatives to `Obj.magic` and correct `Duplicate` link semantics, and also reviewed [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051) requesting additional test coverage

repositories.brief_summary: A major week with the dune_patch OCaml implementation, Lev/Async_io scheduler overhaul, RPC reliability fixes, and a version bump to 3.24.
repositories.new_features_summary: Native OCaml patch implementation eliminates external patch binary dependency; Async_io ported to Lev event loop; trace file backups added.
repositories.new_features:
- **Native OCaml patch action** [#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177): vendors `hannesm/patch` to provide a portable, cross-platform implementation of the patch action, removing the dependency on an external `patch`/`gpatch` binary; fixes [#12232](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12232) and [#7699](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/7699)
- **Async_io ported to Lev** [#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14243): rewrites the async I/O layer to use the bundled `libev`-based Lev event loop, replacing the previous ad-hoc polling approach
- **Alarm-clock thread replaced** [#14329](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14329): scheduler timers now run on the Async_io Lev loop instead of a dedicated polling thread, reducing thread overhead
- **Trace file backups** [#14269](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14269): the default `trace.csexp` is now backed up as `trace.csexp.old` before each build, preserving the previous trace for comparison
- **`dune tools` reference documentation** [#14198](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14198): new comprehensive reference page `doc/reference/dune-tools.rst` documents all `dune tools` subcommands (`exec`, `install`, `which`, `env`) and supported tools
- **Dune version bumped to 3.24** [#14333](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14333): main branch now targets the upcoming 3.24 release after 3.23 release preparations
- **Copilot AI agent integration** [#14321](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14321): adds `.github/copilot-instructions.md` and a `copilot-setup-steps.yml` workflow to configure GitHub Copilot coding agent with the Nix dev shell environment
repositories.activity_summary: Heavy RPC reliability and scheduler refactoring merged alongside the native patch implementation; CI workflows were improved for OxCaml and release branch automation.
repositories.activity:

repositories.discussions_summary: The 3.23.0 release tracker remained the focal discussion, with Windows Ctrl+C regression identified and fixed before release.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211) **3.23.0 release tracker**: active coordination on blockers including Windows Ctrl+C crash [#14302](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14302) and OxCaml revdep failures; nightly build failures from opam generation errors were fixed via [#14270](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14270)
- [#14281](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14281) **Automate cutting releases**: discussion on automating the release process including opam file promotion via `dune build @opam` and the need to run it during release to satisfy the opam metadata diff check introduced in [#14108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14108)

repositories.trends_summary: The scheduler and I/O layer is being systematically migrated to Lev/libev, and AI coding agents (Copilot) are actively contributing PRs with human reviewer oversight.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Lev/libev adoption**: the vendored `libev`-based Lev event loop is progressively replacing bespoke threading in the scheduler — the alarm-clock thread [#14329](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14329) and Async_io [#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14243) both migrated this week, with RPC reliability improvements following
- **Per-module dependency filtering**: [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) is building out a series of baseline cram tests ([#14335](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14335), [#14331](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14331), [#14332](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14332), [#14308](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14308), [#14309](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14309)) as regression guards before landing the per-module `-I` flag filtering [#14186](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14186)
- **AI agent contributions**: GitHub Copilot (`copilot-swe-agent`) is actively opening PRs for CI improvements and bug fixes, with mixed results requiring significant back-and-forth with human reviewers; the team merged the Nix/Copilot setup [#14321](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14321) while closing lower-quality agent PRs
- **Release cadence investment**: multiple CI and automation improvements this week (RC branch release checks, revdeps nix derivation, OxCaml workflow fixes) reflect the team's push toward a sustainable weekly release cadence

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion continued on the Primitive aliases RFC with a positive committee recommendation.
repositories.activity_summary: PR #44 on primitive aliases saw renewed discussion clarifying the scope of alias constraints.
repositories.activity: - **Primitive aliases** RFC [#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) remains open with a `positive-committee-recommendation` label; [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) responded on 2026-04-24 to a prior comment by [Clément Blaudeau](https://github.com/clementblaudeau) clarifying that `with external x = y` constraints would not add meaningful compiler-visible information beyond what existing `with type t = u` constraints already provide.
repositories.discussions_summary: Nailen Matschke addressed Clément Blaudeau's question about `with external` module constraints in RFC #44.
repositories.notable_discussions: - In [#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44), [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) clarified that a hypothetical `with external x = y` constraint form would not allow abstract primitives — any primitive in a signature must either be a fully-specified declaration or an alias of a fully-specified declaration, making two primitives transparently either the same or not. He noted that existing `with type t = u` constraints already handle the only plausible use case, and that the compiler would not infer anything new from `with external x = y`.
repositories.trends_summary: The primitive aliases RFC continues to advance toward resolution with committee support and active design refinement.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The **primitive aliases** proposal [#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) carries a `positive-committee-recommendation` label and is progressing through design discussion, with the recent exchange helping to narrow the scope and justify why a `with external` constraint extension is unnecessary.
repositories.contributors_summary: Nailen Matschke commented on PR #44, responding to Clément Blaudeau's prior review question.
repositories.contributors:
- [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) (Jane Street) authored the **primitive aliases** RFC [#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) and posted a clarifying response on 2026-04-24 addressing [Clément Blaudeau](https://github.com/clementblaudeau)'s question about `with external` module constraints.
- [Clément Blaudeau](https://github.com/clementblaudeau) (Inria/Cryspen) had raised the design question about `with external x = y` that prompted the discussion in [#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44).
- [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) (Jane Street) is credited in the PR body as having reviewed the RFC prior to submission.

repositories.brief_summary: CI workflow added for multi-platform testing and HTTPS links updated across README and opam metadata.
repositories.new_features_summary: GitHub Actions CI added testing Linux/macOS/Windows across OCaml 4.14, 5.4, and 3.08; HTTPS homepage links updated.
repositories.new_features:
- A **GitHub Actions CI workflow** was introduced covering Linux, macOS, and Windows with OCaml versions 4.14, 5.4, and 3.08 (the latter Ubuntu-only), running both `make`-based builds and `opam pin` installs, along with a `topfind` smoke test via a new `toplist.ml` helper script ([#113](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/113))
- **Homepage URL updated to HTTPS** in both `README.md` and the `opam` package metadata, pointing `projects.camlcity.org` links to the secure endpoint ([#83](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/83))
- **`.gitattributes` file added** to enforce consistent `lf` line endings for shell scripts, configure files, and tool scripts, preventing cross-platform line-ending issues ([4c83c36](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/commit/4c83c367b51dda29ad7a00622ca06bff70b74c5b))
repositories.activity_summary: Open PRs cover Windows build fixes, threads exception handling, configure hardening, and OCAMLPATH ordering debate.
repositories.activity:
- **Windows build fixes** in [#112](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/112) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) remain open, addressing `findlib.conf` misgeneration on Cygwin with OCaml 5.0+ (tracked in [ocaml/opam#6732](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6732)) and on MSYS2/mingw-w64 (tracked in [ocaml/opam-repository#28636](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/28636)), plus a fix for an incorrect `META.pkg` check in `ocamlfind install`
- **Exception handling fix** for the `threads` package lookup in [#99](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/99) by [@shym](https://github.com/shym) is awaiting an update to align its CI test with the newly merged workflow from [#113](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/113), as requested by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
- **Configure script hardening** via `set -e` in [#101](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/101) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) remains open; the related fix was cherry-picked into [#112](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/112) with an alternate approach eliminating `find` and `sh` usage
- **OCAMLPATH ordering change** proposed in [#106](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/106) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) (originating from [ocaml/opam-repository#27712](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/27712)) is under discussion, with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) raising concerns that placing `site-lib` before the stdlib directory could shadow compiler `META` files in standard opam switches
- Issue [#68](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/68) reported by [Chen Qi](https://github.com/ChenQi1989) about the HTTP homepage URL was closed after being resolved by the merged [#83](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/83)
repositories.discussions_summary: Active debate on whether OCAMLPATH ordering change in #106 is safe for opam-managed compiler META files.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) raised a concern in [#106](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/106) that giving `site-lib` precedence over the OCaml stdlib directory in `OCAMLPATH` is incorrect for typical opam switches where the compiler's `META` files must not be shadowed; a possible alternative for `ocaml-system` compilers was suggested but considered too involved for the benefit
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) requested in [#99](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/99) that [@shym](https://github.com/shym) update the PR to incorporate the `ocamlfind ocamlopt -config` test into the newly merged CI workflow rather than maintaining a separate workflow file
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on build reliability: CI infrastructure, Windows compatibility, and configure script hardening are all active themes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Build infrastructure investment**: the addition of CI in [#113](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/113) and the ongoing Windows fixes in [#112](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/112) reflect a push to improve reliability across platforms after a period with limited automation
- **Windows and cross-platform portability**: multiple open PRs ([#112](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/112), [#101](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/101)) address shell script portability and platform-specific path handling, suggesting renewed attention to non-Linux environments
repositories.contributors_summary: David Allsopp merged CI and HTTPS PRs; Marek Kubica contributed the HTTPS link update; dra27 also active in PR reviews.
repositories.contributors:

repositories.brief_summary: OxCaml support PRs under active review, roadmap update opened; no merges to master this week.
repositories.activity_summary: ROADMAP update opened and OxCaml kind-annotation PR received design feedback.
repositories.activity:
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) opened [#1421](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1421) to **update ROADMAP.md**, a small documentation-only change that is ready to merge
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) has two ongoing OxCaml PRs receiving active review: **support for kind annotations** ([#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)) and **support for unboxed named types** ([#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)), both currently marked conflicting against master
- Four **OxCaml feature-tracking issues** opened by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) remain open and were updated this week, covering unboxed types ([#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414)), layouts ([#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415)), modalities ([#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416)), and modes ([#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417))
repositories.discussions_summary: Active review on PR #1410 raised questions about kind-product formatting and @@/mod syntax choice.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) left detailed review comments on [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) requesting tests for **kind products** (e.g. `bit32 & float64`) and discussing whether parentheses are needed for compound modifiers such as `(bit32 mod portable) & (float64 mod contended)`, with reference to how `oxcamlformat` handles multi-modifier layouts
- [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) added a drive-by comment on [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) noting the syntax should use **`@@` rather than `mod`** for kind modifiers, pointing to the OxCaml kinds syntax specification
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) also flagged a potential **documentation inconsistency** between `.ml` and `.mli` files in [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410): types without explicit annotations may receive an inferred layout annotation in `cmt` files but not in `cmi` files, which could produce unexpected output when `ml` files are used without accompanying `mli` files
repositories.trends_summary: OxCaml compatibility is the dominant theme, with multiple open PRs and tracking issues for unboxed types, layouts, modalities, and modes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml support** is emerging as a sustained workstream: [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) is driving two open PRs ([#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407), [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)) while [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) has filed four tracking issues ([#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414), [#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415), [#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416), [#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417)) to systematically track the remaining gaps across unboxed types, layouts, modalities, and modes
- Review discussion on [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) is shaping **how OxCaml-specific syntax should be pretty-printed** in generated documentation, with open questions around line-breaking, indentation, and parenthesisation for complex kind expressions
repositories.contributors_summary: art-w drove OxCaml PRs; Jon Ludlam opened a roadmap update; panglesd and Leo White provided reviews.
repositories.contributors:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) authored both open OxCaml PRs: **support for unboxed named types** ([#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)) and **support for kind annotations** ([#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)), and responded to review feedback on [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)

- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) provided detailed review comments on [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) covering test coverage, formatting edge cases, and a potential inconsistency in how layouts appear for `.ml` vs `.mli` inputs
- [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) contributed a targeted review comment on [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) clarifying the correct OxCaml syntax for kind modifiers
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) filed the four OxCaml tracking issues ([#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414), [#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415), [#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416), [#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417)) that were updated this week, helping coordinate the broader OxCaml support effort

repositories.new_features_summary: Seven new OCaml Planet blog posts added, covering GTK4 bindings, pure-OCaml Tailwind CSS, and unikernel research.
repositories.new_features:
- **Seven new OCaml Planet posts** scraped and published to the website across three automated PRs ([#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624), [#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625), [#3626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3626)), bringing the following content to the OCaml community feed:
- **"Tailwind Without Node"** by Thomas Gazagnaire ([bb29ba6](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/commit/bb29ba61aa0ba20f2e2adeb82c79709209f61636)) — describes a pure OCaml implementation of Tailwind CSS v4 that matches the reference binary byte for byte, eliminating the Node.js dependency from OCaml web stacks and enabling browser-side compilation via js_of_ocaml ([#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625))
- **"ocgtk preview1 now on opam"** by Chris Armstrong ([bb29ba6](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/commit/bb29ba61aa0ba20f2e2adeb82c79709209f61636)) — announces ocgtk, a set of GTK4 bindings for OCaml, now available on opam as a preview1 release ([#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624))
- **"Library Operating Systems for the Desktop"** by Thomas Gazagnaire ([b42899a](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/commit/b42899a8f81c2ce6c03f0449e4c73e17a840607b)) — covers the 2025 ICFP paper named Publication of the Year by the Cambridge Ring, detailing how OCaml unikernels came to run the networking stack inside Docker Desktop ([#3626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3626))
- **"Prefetch opam files for day10 –fork"** by Mark Elvers and **"OCaml Weekly News, 21 Apr 2026"** by Caml Weekly News also added this week ([#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625))
- **".plan-26-16: Chennai, Cambridge, Belfast"** by Anil Madhavapeddy covering FP Launchpad at IIT Madras and the Cambridge Ring Hall of Fame added via [#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624)
repositories.activity_summary: One open PR for new platform releases is pending merge, and a bug report was filed about incorrect dates on the news page.
repositories.activity:

repositories.brief_summary: A busy week with 30+ package merges including new AI tooling, email/DNS/MirageOS ecosystem updates, and a large Windows conf-mingw-w64 infrastructure spring-clean.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages this week include a Valkey Eio client, OCaml IDNA library, wire format DSL, slugline slugifier, and NATS protocol client.
repositories.new_features:
- **valkey.0.2.0** — Eio-native OCaml 5 client for the Valkey key-value store with RESP3, TLS, cluster routing and auto-reconnect, merged via [#29783](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29783).
- **idna.0.4.0** — Pure-OCaml IDNA2008 hostname validation, UTS#46, Punycode and Unicode NFC normalization with no C dependencies, merged via [#29801](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29801).
- **wire.0.9.0** — Initial release of a binary wire format DSL with EverParse 3D output, merged via [#29786](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29786).
- **slugline.1.0.0** — First release of a small portable OCaml slugifier, merged via [#29789](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29789).
- **openrouter_api.0.0.1** — OCaml Async-based client for the OpenRouter unified LLM API, merged via [#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745).
- **rescriptdep.0.1.2** — Dependency analyzer for ReScript modules using cmi files, merged via [#29782](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29782).
- **alcobar.0.3.1** — Fork of Crowbar renamed Alcobar, a fuzzing library with Alcotest-compatible API, merged via [#29784](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29784).
- **ppx_mikmatch.1.4** — PPX for matching regular expressions with OCaml patterns via ocaml-re, merged via [#29781](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29781).
- **nats-client.0.0.9** (2 packages) — Lean OCaml NATS protocol client with async support, open for review at [#29806](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29806).
repositories.activity_summary: Over 30 pull requests were merged spanning new AI SDK tooling, major MirageOS ecosystem updates, ATD data format tooling, and extensive Windows conf-mingw-w64 infrastructure work.
repositories.activity:
- **OCaml 5.5.0 beta1** was added to the repository via [#29750](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29750), and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) submitted a batch of OCaml 5.5 compatibility fixes across packages including **mdx**, **ppx_import**, **grenier**, **earlybird**, **goblint**, **charset**, **camldiets**, **bitmasks**, and **abella** via [#29757](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29757), [#29759](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29759), [#29762](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29762), [#29771](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29771), [#29772](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29772), [#29770](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29770), and others.
- **atd.4.2.0** (13 packages) — Major release of the ATD data format tooling adding Java list aliases, OCaml YAML output support, and jsonlike serializers, merged via [#29802](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29802).
- **opentelemetry.0.91** (9 packages) — Updated OpenTelemetry instrumentation suite switching from ocurl to curl dependency, merged via [#29755](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29755).
- **ocaml-ai-sdk.0.3** (2 packages) — Ahrefs OCaml AI SDK provider abstraction updated with Anthropic native structured outputs, HTTP timeouts, and new Claude model catalog, merged via [#29761](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29761).
- **colombe.0.13.0** (6 packages) — SMTP protocol suite update adding new **msendmail** package and STARTTLS improvements, merged via [#29766](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29766).
- **dns.10.2.5** (13 packages) — DNS library security fix using constant-time comparison for TSIG MAC verification, merged via [#29779](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29779).
- **bos.0.3.0** — Daniel Bünzli's OS interaction library updated with XDG directory support, realpath, and deadlock fix, with revdep constraints for packages depending on the dropped `rresult` dep, merged via [#29791](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29791).
- **cmdliner.2.1.1** — Bug-fix release with bash-completion improvements and Windows path fixes, merged via [#29788](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29788).
- **yamlx.0.2.0** and **yamlx.0.3.0** — Two rapid releases of the pure-OCaml YAML parser with improved comment handling and block scalar output, merged via [#29774](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29774) and [#29794](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29794).
- **conf-mingw-w64 spring-clean** — [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) mechanised a comprehensive cleanup of ~70 Windows conf-mingw-w64 packages hardening pkgconf calls, adding consistent metadata, and ensuring C compiler dependencies, merged via [#29795](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29795).
- **dkim.0.11.0** (4 packages), **uspf.0.2.1** (3 packages), **mrmime.0.7.2**, **prettym.0.0.5**, **mcrunch.0.0.2**, **flux.0.0.1~beta5**, and **mirage.4.10.6** represent continued MirageOS/Robur ecosystem activity merged throughout the week.
- **liquidsoap.2.4.4** (4 packages) — Swiss-army knife multimedia streaming suite updated, merged via [#29754](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29754).
- **elpi.3.7.1**, **earlybird.1.3.6**, **tracy-client.0.7.1**, **menhir-lsp.0.3.2**, **interval-map.0.2.1**, **ocaml-version.4.1.0**, **dockerfile.8.3.6**, and **base.v0.14.4/v0.15.2/v0.16.5** were also merged during the week.
- **lwt.6.1.2** remains open pending test failures on macOS and FreeBSD, at [#29752](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29752).
- **bin_there.0.2.1a1** and **ppx_bin_there.0.2.1a1** for serialization with shared-value support are open awaiting CI fixes, at [#29796](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29796).
repositories.discussions_summary: Notable discussions covered a Windows MSYS2 depext expansion bug and ongoing OCaml 5.5 readiness tracking.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **MSYS2 depext variable expansion bug** — Issue [#29749](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29749) reported that `%{msys2:package-prefix}%` variables were not being expanded when passed to pacman, causing spurious reinstallations; closed as resolved after [#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579) was partially reverted via [#29751](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29751).
- **OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness** — Issue [#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463) continues to track platform tool and ecosystem package compatibility for the upcoming OCaml 5.5 release, with active updates as the beta1 was published.
- **GCC 15 breaking OCaml 5.0.0 on Arch Linux** — Issue [#28250](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/28250) resurfaced with discussion on patches that exist in ocurrent/opam-repository but have not been upstreamed, with [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) and [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers) noting that [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) had a plan for upstreaming them.
- **jsonschema-validation upper bound** — CI revealed that `jsonschema-validation.0.1.0` broke when **idna.0.4.0** removed the `is_valid_hostname` function; [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) quickly submitted [#29804](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29804) with a constraint fix, which was merged same-day.
repositories.trends_summary: The week saw a strong AI/LLM tooling push alongside continued MirageOS ecosystem activity and proactive OCaml 5.5 compatibility work.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **AI/LLM tooling expansion** — Both **ocaml-ai-sdk.0.3** ([#29761](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29761)) and **openrouter_api.0.0.1** ([#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745)) landed this week, reflecting growing interest in OCaml bindings for LLM provider APIs.
- **OCaml 5.5 readiness wave** — [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) submitted a coordinated batch of compatibility constraint fixes ahead of the 5.5.0 beta1, a pattern seen in previous OCaml release cycles.
- **Windows infrastructure investment** — The large [#29795](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29795) conf-mingw-w64 spring-clean is explicitly preparatory work for adding MSYS2 UCRT64 support, continuing steady improvements to Windows packaging.
- **MirageOS/Robur ecosystem momentum** — [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) contributed multiple packages (colombe, uspf, mcrunch, flux, dkim, mrmime, prettym) reflecting active development in the unikernel/networking space.

repositories.brief_summary: Browser compatibility discussion prompted a new Babel documentation PR; CI updated with actions/checkout v6.
repositories.activity_summary: New documentation PR for Babel-based older browser targeting opened; dependabot bumped CI checkout action.
repositories.activity:
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened [#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216) — a **new manual chapter** documenting how to target older browsers using Babel and core-js to downlevel js_of_ocaml's ES2020 output, with an end-to-end cram test (`compiler/tests-jsoo/babel-downlevel/`) gated behind a `@runtest-babel-downlevel` alias so it stays out of the default `@runtest`; the PR also fixes `ECMASCRIPT.md` (async/await are ES2017, not ES2016) and adds Promise and template literals (ES2015) to the feature table. This PR is open and awaiting review.
- **Dependabot** merged [#2214](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2214), bumping `actions/checkout` from v5 to v6 in the CI workflow.
- The long-running draft PR [#833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/833) — an **alternative interface for jsooTop** (`JsooTopWrapped`, `JsooTopAsynchronous`) originally developed for the OCaml MOOC — received an update to its metadata this week; it remains a WIP draft.
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on issue #2215 explored older browser and quickjs compatibility, leading to the Babel documentation PR.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) opened [#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215) reporting that js_of_ocaml's use of **ES6 classes** and `TextDecoder` breaks on iOS 16.7.5 / Safari and Firefox ESR 45.9.0, requesting that generated code either detect missing features at load time or expose missing ones so applications can show a meaningful error.
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) responded that **optional chaining** (added recently) will be an additional compatibility concern, recommended Babel as the practical path forward, and noted that bundling (esbuild or similar) is needed to resolve `require`/`import` after transpilation — directly leading to opening [#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216).
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) also pointed to [#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831) as relevant prior work, and noted that **quickjs support** could be contributed via a new CI job, with reference to an existing njs fork that may have added TextDecoder support.
repositories.trends_summary: Older browser and non-browser JS runtime compatibility is emerging as a focus area for the project.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The discussion in [#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215) and the resulting PR [#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216) reflect a pattern of **increasing use of modern JavaScript features** (ES6 classes, optional chaining, `TextDecoder`) in js_of_ocaml output, raising questions about compatibility with older browsers and alternative runtimes like quickjs.
- There is interest in documenting and testing **Babel-based downleveling workflows** as the recommended path for users who need to support older environments, rather than maintaining internal polyfills or fallbacks.
repositories.contributors_summary: Two contributors active: @hhugo opened the Babel doc PR and engaged in compatibility discussions; Stéphane Glondu filed the browser compatibility issue.
repositories.contributors:
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened the documentation PR [#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216) for Babel-based browser targeting, provided detailed technical responses in [#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215) including pointing to existing prior work [#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831), and updated the draft PR [#833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/833).
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) filed the feature request [#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215) with a concrete reproduction (iOS 16.7.5 Safari, Firefox ESR 45.9.0), participated in the follow-up discussion, and expressed interest in quickjs support.

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with mixed-block representation overhaul, Flambda2 optimizations, 5.4 merge progress, and a documentation terminology cleanup.
repositories.new_features_summary: Mixed block version bumped to v6; uniform blocks for all-value mixed records; caml_modify skipped for non_pointers; int comparison codegen improved.
repositories.new_features:
- **All-value/void mixed records and variants** now compiled to uniform blocks instead of mixed blocks, enabling polymorphic comparison and marshalling on these types and removing the 254-field limit; the mixed block version was bumped to v6 ([#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- **`[@@flatten_floats]` attribute** is now required on records mixing `float` and `float64` fields to preserve the flattened representation; without the attribute the compiler will no longer flatten these floats ([#5795](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5795) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- **`caml_modify` skipped for `non_pointer` fields** in mixed blocks, avoiding unnecessary write-barrier overhead for GC-opaque fields ([#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- **Improved codegen for integer comparisons**: plain bit-result comparisons now use `xor` before comparison instead of zero-extending after, and three-way comparisons use a `select (x >= y) (x > y) -1` form, reducing chain latency and instruction count ([#5738](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5738) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet))
- **Flambda2 switch-table optimization extended to naked numbers and symbols**, covering patterns like variants returning `float32` or `int32` constants ([#3242](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3242) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell))
- **`Random.State` functions** annotated as accepting the state `@ local`, allowing stack-allocated random states without heap allocation ([#5908](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5908) by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic))
- **`Dummy_use` operation removed** from the register allocator backend; the operation was found to be ineffective at keeping temporaries live, simplifying the backend ([#5614](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5614) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet))
- **Syntax coloring added to the fexpr printer** for Flambda2 IR dumps, making debug output easier to read in terminals ([#5925](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5925) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp))
- **Diamond-aware mode hints** improve error messages when a mode Meet or Join involves parallel lattice elements, correctly attributing the faulty branch in error output ([#5858](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5858) by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges))
- **Documentation: mutable field modalities** are now documented in the modes syntax reference, explaining the implicit `@@ global many dynamic` modality and how explicit modalities compose with it ([#5897](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5897) by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs), merged via [#5897](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5897))
- **Documentation: `with-kind` syntax rationale** added, explaining how `type t with ty @@ modality` behaves for mode-crossing ([#5896](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5896) by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs))
- **Public docs terminology cleanup**: removed monadic/comonadic language ([#5893](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5893)), replaced "upstream compiler" with "stock OCaml" ([#5894](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5894)), fixed minor issues in extension docs ([#5898](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5898)), updated C macro example to `Assert_mixed_block_layout_v6` ([#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774)) — all by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs)
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs opened on preemption, copy propagation, .cmx efficiency, and legacy mode syntax removal; 5.4 merge work continued across typing, testsuite, and stdlib.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened: Preemptible fibers** ([#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913) by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark)), building on the initial preemption PR ([#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881)) to add preemptible-fiber abstractions; the preemption PR itself received an arm64 port and 256-bit register test validation this week
- **Newly opened: Remove `unique_` and `once_` legacy mode prefix syntax** ([#5931](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5931) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)), converting all test files to use the modern `@ mode` syntax
- **Newly opened: Efficiency improvements for `.cmx` export** ([#5929](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5929) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)), avoiding traversal and rewrite of non-exported code and reducing unnecessary `Name_occurrences` construction during CMX export
- **Newly opened: Copy propagation pass** for linscan and greedy allocators ([#5914](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5914) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)), deleting moves and temporary registers in single-block ranges, reducing moves by ~3-5%
- **Newly opened: `regalloc.exe` fix for copy propagation** ([#5930](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5930) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)), making the `-copy-propagation` flag actually run the pass
- **Newly opened: Speculative inlining fix for lifted constants** ([#5917](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5917) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)), preventing the inliner from over-counting gains when code is merely lifted rather than simplified
- **Newly opened: Fix kind annotation check for existential types in patterns** ([#5935](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5935) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)), fixing a soundness bug where kind annotations on existential type variables could silently narrow their declared kind, allowing programs that could segfault
- **Newly opened: `float64` records as mixed blocks with `[@@represent_as_float_array]`** ([#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)), converting all-`float64` records from float-array blocks to mixed blocks for uniformity, while providing `[@@represent_as_float_array]` for legacy compatibility
- **Newly opened: `reaper` bug fix for kind rewriting** ([#5934](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5934) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)), a one-line fix for a regression introduced in [#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787)
- **Newly opened: More Flambda2 profiling for CMX loading** ([#5910](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5910) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)) and initial type provision when defining variables ([#5922](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5922) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)), both targeting compilation speed
- **Newly opened: User documentation for `borrow_` operator** ([#5928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5928) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)), covering borrow regions, properties of borrowed values, valid positions, and nested borrowing
- **Merged: Effects/continuations correctness** — fixed `caml_continuation_use_noexc` not being called when resuming unhandled continuations ([#5906](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5906) by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark)), and `epoll_wait` no longer fatal-errors on `EINTR` in the tick thread ([#5904](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5904) by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark))
- **Merged: Flambda2 compilation speed improvements** — removed `imported_names` from the typing environment (showed up in profiles during CMX loading) ([#5886](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5886) by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls)), fixed physical equality mistakes in `Type_grammar` causing excess allocation during `apply_renaming` ([#5901](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5901) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell))
- **Merged: Flambda2 correctness fixes** — fixed bug in `meet` for array element kinds causing loss of element kind information ([#5771](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5771) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)), fixed `lambda_to_flambda` generating unnecessary `%is_int` switches for GADT matches with proven-impossible constructors ([#5879](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5879) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp))
- **Merged: reaper compute code size when rebuilding** ([#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs))
- **Merged: `caml_raise_async` bug fix** — was incorrectly calling `caml_raise` instead ([#5846](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5846) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes))
- **Merged: `Eval` metaprogramming** — moved `Eval.use_existing_compilerlibs_state_for_artifacts` to `Opttoploop` to fix MDX dependency issues ([#5902](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5902) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell))
- **Merged: Sort variable IDs in `.cmi` files** now use negative IDs to avoid collisions when loading, consistent with how type variable IDs are handled ([#5663](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5663) by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian))
- **5.4 merge progress**: labeled tuples support landed ([#5692](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5692) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)), `Iarray` module re-oxidised ([#5882](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5882)), effect backtrace improvements ([#5924](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5924) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)), type checker fixes ([#5859](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5859)), shape mechanism fix ([#5888](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5888)), and multiple testsuite fixes ([#5911](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5911), [#5915](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5915), [#5919](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5919), [#5920](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5920), [#5926](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5926))
- **Ongoing: Layout polymorphism** — `Add env_alloc_mode to Tpat_fun_layout` merged ([#5662](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5662) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)); layout polymorphic functions draft ([#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870) by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr)); persistent modules as `static` ([#5834](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5834) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn))
- **Ongoing: Backend improvements** — X86 push/pop spilling pass under review ([#5635](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5635) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)), SSA CFG variant draft ([#5810](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5810)), small int machtype refactor ([#5664](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5664) by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4))
- **Ongoing: Metaprogramming** — first-class modules in quotes ([#5884](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5884) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski)), basic functorization support draft ([#5903](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5903) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)), runtime eval in expect-tests draft ([#5933](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5933) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski))
- **Ongoing: Typing improvements** — `argument_types_useful` check improvement ([#5760](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5760) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)), Patricia trees using GADTs to encode invariants ([#5899](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5899) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)), zero-alloc on higher-order function arguments ([#5493](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5493) by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian))
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion on preemption arm64 porting, existential kind annotation soundness, and .cmx export API design.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **preemption PR** ([#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881)) saw active discussion with [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) noting that the preemption256 test validates that 256-bit YMM registers are correctly saved across preemptions (confirmed by stepping through `caml_preempt` in gdb), and discussion of whether `expect_asm` tests could be used to lock down the assembly output
- **Kind annotation safety for existentials** ([#5935](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5935)) prompted detailed discussion between [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) and [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) about the conditions under which existential types need checking even when not explicitly named, and about a subtle `non_float` error arising from `apply_or_null_r` separability constraints
- **`.cmx` export efficiency** ([#5929](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5929)) raised API design questions from [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) about whether the fold-based approach could cause de-duplication issues, with a suggestion to expose `free_imported_function_slots_and_value_slots` rather than folding all free names
- **Mutable field modalities** documentation ([#5897](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5897)) prompted a discussion between [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) about how explicit modalities interact with the implicit mutable-field modality, leading to an investigation and documentation update
- **Data flow and variant unboxing poison values** ([#5923](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5923), opened by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)) identified a case where data flow analysis cannot see through alias relationships when poison values are involved, preventing switch simplification
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained push on mixed block/unboxed type representation changes, public documentation quality, and 5.4 upstream merge readiness.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Mixed block representation overhaul** is progressing systematically: uniform blocks for all-value mixed records ([#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774)), `float64`-only records moving to mixed blocks ([#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796)), and `[@@flatten_floats]` as an opt-in escape hatch ([#5795](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5795)) — all from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), pointing toward a coherent and more uniform memory representation model
- **Public documentation terminology standardization** saw a batch of PRs ([#5893](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5893), [#5894](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5894), [#5896](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5896), [#5897](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5897), [#5898](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5898)) from [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) removing internal compiler terminology (monadic/comonadic, "upstream") from user-facing docs, suggesting a maturing documentation effort aimed at external users
- **Compilation speed investment** is visible across multiple concurrent efforts: `imported_names` removal ([#5886](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5886)), physical equality fixes ([#5901](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5901)), `.cmx` export optimization ([#5929](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5929)), and initial type provision for variables ([#5922](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5922))
- **Effects/preemption infrastructure** is advancing in parallel tracks: correctness fixes to the continuation stack ([#5906](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5906)), tick thread robustness ([#5904](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5904)), and preemptible fibers as a new abstraction ([#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913))

repositories.brief_summary: Expanded syntax-doc support for scannable axes and merged compiler changes from 5.2.0minus-37 including -Ix flag handling.
repositories.new_features_summary: Syntax-doc now covers all scannable axis annotations; Merlin handles both -I and -Ix compiler flags.
repositories.new_features:
- **Syntax documentation for scannable axis annotations** expanded to cover all variants (`non_pointer`, `non_pointer64`, `non_float`, `separable`, `maybe_separable`, `non_null`, `maybe_null`), each with descriptive hover text and a link to the OxCaml kinds/syntax documentation ([#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235))
- **`-Ix` flag support** added so Merlin correctly parses both `-I` and `-Ix` compiler load path flags, with a new test suite (`tests/test-dirs/ix-flag.t`) verifying behavior via `.merlin` files and command-line flags ([#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234))
- **`Scannable_axis_annotation` AST node** introduced in `browse_raw` to give Merlin precise location tracking for individual scannable axis modifiers, enabling accurate cursor-position-based documentation lookup ([#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235))
- **Load path initialisation refactored** in `load_path.ml` to simplify the entry-reuse logic, replacing nested `loop_unchanged`/`loop_changed` helpers with cleaner `get_new_dirs`/`process_entries`/`create_dirs` functions ([#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234))
repositories.activity_summary: Two PRs merged on 2026-04-20: compiler sync from 5.2.0minus-37 and improved scannable-axes syntax documentation.
repositories.activity:
- **Compiler sync** from `5.2.0minus-37` merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), bringing upstream OCaml compiler changes into Merlin and resolving conflicts around the `-I` vs `-Ix` include flag distinction introduced in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654) ([#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234))
- **Scannable axes syntax-doc improvements** merged, adding hover documentation for the full set of kind modifiers in OxCaml's kind system ([#235](https://github.comlin/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235))
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to keep Merlin's syntax-doc feature aligned with OxCaml language extensions as the kind system evolves.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Incremental OxCaml language extension coverage** in Merlin's syntax-doc feature continues, with kind modifiers and scannable axes joining the set of constructs that surface documentation on hover ([#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235))
- **Regular compiler merges** from upstream OxCaml snapshots (e.g. `5.2.0minus-37`) keep Merlin's parser and load-path handling in sync with compiler changes ([#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234))
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson authored both PRs; @dkalinichenko-js reviewed and approved both.
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) authored both PRs: the compiler sync ([#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234)) and the scannable axes syntax-doc improvement ([#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235)), including the `-Ix` test suite, load-path refactor, and `Scannable_axis_annotation` AST additions
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) reviewed both PRs, requesting tests for `-I` vs `-Ix` handling and clarifying the test coverage situation for scannable axes before approving and merging

repositories.brief_summary: Three long-standing PRs closed after direct pushes to main; new dune 3.22.2+ox draft PR opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: New oxcaml-patch-guards mechanism and updated patched packages pushed to main; dune 3.22.2+ox in progress.
repositories.new_features:
- The **`oxcaml-patch-guards` mechanism** for allowing multiple versions of upstream patched packages to coexist in an OxCaml switch was pushed to main, as implemented in [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27). This replaces the monolithic `conflicts` clause in `ocaml-variants.5.2.0+ox` with a generated `oxcaml-patch-guards` package containing disjunctive `depends` clauses, enabling packages like `tsdl` to install alongside upgraded patched dependencies.
- **Updated patched upstream packages** added to the repository: `ocamlbuild.0.16.1+ox`, `sedlex.3.7+ox`, `topkg.1.1.1+ox`, `uutf.1.0.4+ox`, `ctypes.0.24.0+ox`, and `ctypes-foreign.0.24.0+ox`, enabling installation of packages such as `tsdl.1.2.0` ([#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27), [#23](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/23)).
- **Package coinstallability fixes** including zarith dependency corrections and lwt 6 compatibility alongside the OxCaml compiler were pushed to main ([#32](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/32)).
- **`dune 3.22.2+ox`** packaging is in progress in a new draft PR [#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39), though blocked on an `odoc` CI failure.
repositories.activity_summary: New draft PR for dune 3.22.2+ox opened; three older PRs closed after direct commits to main by David Allsopp.
repositories.activity:

- **PR [#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36)** by [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou), updating the bootstrap compiler to OCaml 5.4.1 in the `ocaml-variants` opam file, remains open but has a merge conflict.

repositories.discussions_summary: The odoc CI failure is a recurring blocker, affecting both PR #39 and the wider CI stabilisation effort.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **`odoc` CI breakage** is an ongoing blocker: PR [#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39) was opened specifically hoping to resolve it via a newer `dune` version, but the CI failure on `odoc` caused [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) to immediately switch the PR to draft.
- PR [#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) by [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) attempted CI stabilisation through fixes for `odoc`, `sherlodoc`, `checkseum` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29747](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29747)), and a macOS build issue, as well as pinning `lwt` and `utop` to in-repo versions — ultimately closed without merge.
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) noted in [#32](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/32) that `obuild` had no relevance to OxCaml and was removed; the remaining coinstallability fixes were combined with the patched-package overhaul from [#27](https://github.comml/opam-repository/issues/27).
repositories.trends_summary: Package management infrastructure is being consolidated through direct pushes to main rather than PR merges.
repositories.emerging_trends:

- **CI stability around `odoc`** is a recurring obstacle: both a dedicated CI fix PR ([#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38)) and a `dune` upgrade PR ([#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39)) have been blocked or rendered ineffective by `odoc` build failures.
- **Dune package management compatibility** remains an active theme, with open PRs for `odoc-parser` ([#22](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/22)) and `ocamlbuild` ([#21](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/21)) awaiting merge.
