group_summaries.brief_summary: 30+ opam packages merged including OCaml 5.5.0 beta1 and new AI/LLM tooling; ocaml.org published seven Planet posts on Tailwind CSS and GTK4.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New opam packages include Valkey Eio client, IDNA library, NATS client, and ocgtk GTK4 bindings preview announced on ocaml.org.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29783](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29783) valkey.0.2.0** — Eio-native OCaml 5 client for the Valkey key-value store with RESP3, TLS, cluster routing, and auto-reconnect, submitted by [Avi Fenesh](https://github.com/avifenesh).
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29801](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29801) idna.0.4.0** — Pure-OCaml IDNA2008 hostname validation, UTS#46, Punycode and Unicode NFC normalization with no C dependencies, submitted by [Yuriy Krasilnikov](https://github.com/YuriyKrasilnikov).
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29786](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29786) wire.0.9.0** — Initial release of a binary wire format DSL with EverParse 3D output, by [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht).
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29789](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29789) slugline.1.0.0** — First release of a small portable OCaml slugifier, by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw).
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745) openrouter_api.0.0.1** — OCaml Async-based client for the OpenRouter unified LLM API, by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret).
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29782](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29782) rescriptdep.0.1.2** — Dependency analyzer for ReScript modules using cmi files, submitted by [woonki](https://github.com/mununki).
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29784](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29784) alcobar.0.3.1** — Fork of Crowbar renamed Alcobar, a fuzzing library with Alcotest-compatible API, by [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht).
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29781](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29781) ppx_mikmatch.1.4** — PPX for matching regular expressions with OCaml patterns via ocaml-re, by [Leonardo Santos](https://github.com/zazedd).
- **[ocaml/opam-repository#29806](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29806) nats-client.0.0.9** (2 packages) — Lean OCaml NATS protocol client with async support, open for review, by [Heb](https://github.com/Hebilicious).
- **[ocaml/ocaml.org#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624) ocgtk preview1** — GTK4 bindings for OCaml now available on opam, announced via Planet post by Chris Armstrong.
- **[ocaml/ocaml.org#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625) "Tailwind Without Node"** — Blog post by [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht) describing a pure-OCaml Tailwind CSS v4 implementation that eliminates the Node.js dependency, with browser-side compilation via js_of_ocaml.
- **[ocaml/ocaml.org#3626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3626) "Library Operating Systems for the Desktop"** — Blog post covering the 2025 ICFP paper on OCaml unikernels running Docker Desktop's networking stack, named Publication of the Year by the Cambridge Ring.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **[ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository)** processed 30+ pull requests this week, including [OCaml 5.5.0 beta1](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29750), multiple new package releases, a 13-package ATD major version ([ocaml/opam-repository#29802](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29802)), and a large Windows conf-mingw-w64 infrastructure cleanup (~70 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29795](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29795)).
- **[ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org)** had lighter code activity, with three automated OCaml Planet scraping PRs merged ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3626)) adding seven new community blog posts, and one platform releases PR pending ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3621](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3621)).
- **27 contributors** were active across the group, with the majority of activity concentrated in opam-repository.
- **Security** received attention via a constant-time TSIG MAC verification fix in dns.10.2.5 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29779](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29779)) and a Windows depext variable-expansion bug fix ([ocaml/opam-repository#29751](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29751)).
- **AI/LLM tooling** continued to expand with ocaml-ai-sdk.0.3 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29761](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29761)) and openrouter_api.0.0.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745)) both merging this week.
group_summaries.activity_summary: OCaml 5.5.0 beta1 added to opam-repository; ATD 4.2.0, dns security fix, bos/cmdliner updates, and Windows infrastructure work merged alongside seven Planet posts.
group_summaries.activity:
- **OCaml 5.5.0 beta1** added to opam-repository via [ocaml/opam-repository#29750](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29750); [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) submitted coordinated compatibility fixes across **mdx**, **ppx_import**, **grenier**, **earlybird**, **goblint**, **charset**, **camldiets**, **bitmasks**, and **abella** (see [#29757](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29757), [#29759](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29759), [#29762](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29762), [#29771](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29771), [#29772](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29772), [#29770](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29770)).
- **atd.4.2.0** (13 packages) merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29802](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29802), adding Java list aliases, OCaml YAML output, and jsonlike serializers, by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon).
- **opentelemetry.0.91** (9 packages) merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29755](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29755), switching from ocurl to curl dependency, by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube).
- **ocaml-ai-sdk.0.3** merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29761](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29761), adding Anthropic native structured outputs, HTTP timeouts, and a new Claude model catalog, by [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy).
- **colombe.0.13.0** (6 packages) merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29766](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29766), adding **msendmail** and STARTTLS improvements, by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure).
- **dns.10.2.5** (13 packages) merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29779](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29779) with a constant-time comparison security fix for TSIG MAC verification, by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm).
- **bos.0.3.0** merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29791](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29791) with XDG directory support, realpath, and a deadlock fix; includes revdep constraints for packages that depended on the dropped `rresult` dep, by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli).
- **cmdliner.2.1.1** merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29788](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29788) with bash-completion and Windows path fixes, by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli).
- **yamlx.0.2.0** and **yamlx.0.3.0** merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29774](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29774) and [ocaml/opam-repository#29794](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29794) with improved comment handling and block scalar output, by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon).
- **conf-mingw-w64 spring-clean** (~70 packages) merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29795](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29795), hardening pkgconf calls and adding consistent metadata as preparatory work for MSYS2 UCRT64 support, by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27).
- **MirageOS/Robur ecosystem**: **dkim.0.11.0** (4 packages), **uspf.0.2.1** (3 packages), **mrmime.0.7.2**, **prettym.0.0.5**, **mcrunch.0.0.2**, **flux.0.0.1~beta5**, and **mirage.4.10.6** all merged, primarily from [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) and [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir).
- **liquidsoap.2.4.4** (4 packages) merged via [ocaml/opam-repository#29754](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29754) by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots).
- **MSYS2 depext bug** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29749](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29749)) resolved by partially reverting [ocaml/opam-repository#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579) via [ocaml/opam-repository#29751](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29751).
- **lwt.6.1.2** remains open pending test failures on macOS and FreeBSD at [ocaml/opam-repository#29752](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29752).
- **OCaml Planet scraping** merged three PRs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3626)) adding seven new community posts; recurring scrape errors for several feeds (mega-nerd, 0branch, mirage, ocaml-book, dinosaure, emilpriver, jonludlam, patricoferris, and multiple YouTube playlists) may need attention.
- **Platform releases PR** ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3621](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3621)) adding 1321 lines across 13 files remains open pending merge.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Thomas Gazagnaire** ([Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht)) is active across both repositories: released **wire.0.9.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29786](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29786)) and **alcobar.0.3.1** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29784](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29784)) in opam-repository, and authored the "Tailwind Without Node" blog post published on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625)) describing a pure-OCaml Tailwind CSS v4 implementation.
- **Mark Elvers** ([Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers)) contributed **dockerfile.8.3.6** and **ocaml-version.4.1.0** to opam-repository and also published a blog post ("Prefetch opam files for day10 –fork") on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625)).
- **Anil Madhavapeddy** ([Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm)) reviewed and merged several opam-repository PRs and also had a blog post (".plan-26-16: Chennai, Cambridge, Belfast") published on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624)), covering the FP Launchpad at IIT Madras and the Cambridge Ring Hall of Fame.
- **ocgtk preview1** — Chris Armstrong's GTK4 bindings for OCaml were announced in an OCaml Planet post ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624)); the corresponding opam package publication is the natural downstream step in the ecosystem pipeline.
- **MirageOS ecosystem coverage** spans both repositories: [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) and [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) released multiple MirageOS/Robur packages in opam-repository, while the "Library Operating Systems for the Desktop" ICFP paper was published on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3626)), providing community context for the same body of work.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **MSYS2 depext variable expansion bug** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29749](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29749)) — `%{msys2:package-prefix}%` variables were not expanded when passed to pacman, causing spurious reinstallations; resolved by partially reverting [ocaml/opam-repository#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579) via [ocaml/opam-repository#29751](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29751).
- **OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463)) — tracking issue continues to receive updates as the beta1 was published and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) submitted a batch of compatibility constraint fixes.
- **GCC 15 breaking OCaml 5.0.0 on Arch Linux** ([ocaml/opam-repository#28250](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/28250)) — resurfaced with discussion from [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) and [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers) noting patches exist in ocurrent/opam-repository but have not been upstreamed; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) has a plan for upstreaming them.
- **jsonschema-validation upper bound breakage** — CI revealed that `jsonschema-validation.0.1.0` was broken by the removal of `is_valid_hostname` in **idna.0.4.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29801](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29801)); [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) quickly submitted a constraint fix in [ocaml/opam-repository#29804](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29804), merged same-day.
- **Wrong dates on ocaml.org news page** ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3627](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3627)) — [@756D61](https://github.com/756D61) filed a bug report on 2026-04-26 flagging a potential date parsing or rendering issue affecting how news items are displayed to visitors.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **AI/LLM tooling expansion in OCaml** — Both **ocaml-ai-sdk.0.3** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29761](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29761)) and **openrouter_api.0.0.1** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745)) landed this week, reflecting growing interest in OCaml clients for LLM provider APIs.
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility wave** — [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) coordinated a batch of constraint fixes ahead of the 5.5.0 beta1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29750](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29750)), a pattern seen in previous OCaml release cycles.
- **Windows infrastructure investment** — The conf-mingw-w64 spring-clean ([ocaml/opam-repository#29795](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29795)) is explicitly preparatory work for MSYS2 UCRT64 support, continuing steady improvements to Windows packaging across ~70 packages.
- **MirageOS/Robur ecosystem momentum** — [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) contributed multiple packages (colombe, uspf, mcrunch, flux, dkim, mrmime, prettym) alongside MirageOS academic recognition via the "Library Operating Systems for the Desktop" ICFP paper on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3626)).
- **Automated Planet scraping feed health** — Recurring errors for several feeds (mega-nerd, 0branch, mirage, ocaml-book, dinosaure, emilpriver, jonludlam, patricoferris) across all three scrape PRs this week ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625), [ocaml/ocaml.org#3626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3626)) suggest these sources may need maintenance or removal.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune targets 3.24 with Lev scheduler overhaul and native OCaml patch; OxCaml, Windows portability, and indexer scalability are cross-cutting themes.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune gains native OCaml patch and trace backups; merlin adds pointer-based indexes; ocamlfind gains multi-platform CI workflow.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Native OCaml patch action** ([ocaml/dune#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177)): vendors `hannesm/patch` to replace the external `patch`/`gpatch` binary dependency, fixing portability issues [ocaml/dune#12232](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12232) and [ocaml/dune#7699](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/7699)
- **Async_io ported to Lev** ([ocaml/dune#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14243)): rewrites the async I/O layer to use the bundled `libev`-based Lev event loop, with the alarm-clock thread also migrated ([ocaml/dune#14329](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14329))
- **Trace file backups** ([ocaml/dune#14269](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14269)): `trace.csexp` is now backed up as `trace.csexp.old` before each build
- **`dune tools` reference documentation** ([ocaml/dune#14198](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14198)): new reference page documents all `dune tools` subcommands (`exec`, `install`, `which`, `env`)
- **Dune version bumped to 3.24** ([ocaml/dune#14333](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14333)): main branch targets the upcoming 3.24 release after 3.23 preparations
- **Index pointer system for ocaml-index** ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)): merged indexes now store pointers to constituent index files rather than embedding data inline, reducing file sizes; adds `Outdated_store` exception and `is_on_disk` predicate to the `Granular_marshal` API
- **MenhirLib snapshot refreshed** ([ocaml/merlin#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058)): vendored parser updated using the upstream `--dump-menhirLib` flag, resolving copy-rule failures with recent menhir versions
- **GitHub Actions CI for ocamlfind** ([ocaml/ocamlfind#113](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/113)): covers Linux, macOS, and Windows with OCaml 4.14, 5.4, and 3.08, including `make`-based builds, `opam pin` installs, and a `topfind` smoke test
- **Copilot AI agent integration for dune** ([ocaml/dune#14321](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14321)): adds `.github/copilot-instructions.md` and a `copilot-setup-steps.yml` workflow to configure GitHub Copilot with the Nix dev shell environment
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) had the most activity: native OCaml patch implementation merged ([ocaml/dune#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177)), Async_io ported to Lev ([ocaml/dune#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14243)), multiple RPC reliability fixes, and a version bump to 3.24 ([ocaml/dune#14333](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14333))
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) merged two PRs: pointer-based index format ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) and menhirLib snapshot update ([ocaml/merlin#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058)); one PR for LRU memory bounding ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)) remains open
- [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind) merged a multi-platform CI workflow ([ocaml/ocamlfind#113](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/113)) and HTTPS link update ([ocaml/ocamlfind#83](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/83)); Windows build fixes ([ocaml/ocamlfind#112](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/112)) and OCAMLPATH ordering ([ocaml/ocamlfind#106](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/106)) remain open
- [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) had no merges; two OxCaml PRs ([ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410), [ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)) are under active review alongside four OxCaml tracking issues
- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) saw one new PR for `chdir` removal ([ocaml/opam#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)); two new Windows-specific bugs filed ([ocaml/opam#6909](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6909), [ocaml/opam#6908](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6908)); ongoing work on cmdliner completions, Nushell support, and disk usage reduction
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) received only an automated Nix flake lock update ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)) picking up the latest merlin and nixpkgs revisions
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) opened a Babel downleveling documentation PR ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)) driven by a browser compatibility report ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215))
- [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format) and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) had no recorded activity this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: RPC and scheduler refactoring dominated dune; opam Windows bugs filed; odoc OxCaml PRs under design review; js_of_ocaml browser compatibility discussed.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) landed a series of RPC reliability fixes in dune: cancel pending requests ([ocaml/dune#14334](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14334)), server-side cancellation ([ocaml/dune#14312](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14312)), wait for listener shutdown ([ocaml/dune#14322](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14322)), and fatal bind failure handling ([ocaml/dune#14330](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14330))
- [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) merged a Windows Ctrl+C shutdown fix ([ocaml/dune#14316](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14316)) and is building a baseline cram test series ([ocaml/dune#14335](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14335)) ahead of per-module `-I` flag filtering ([ocaml/dune#14186](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14186))
- [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) coordinated the 3.23.0 release, fixed OxCaml CI workflows ([ocaml/dune#14299](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14299)), updated OxCaml to 5.2.0minus-31 ([ocaml/dune#14305](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14305)), and fixed fsevents warning on macOS ([ocaml/dune#14246](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14246))
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) added release CI checks running on `*-rc` branches ([ocaml/dune#14271](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14271)) and filed four OxCaml tracking issues for odoc ([ocaml/odoc#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414), [ocaml/odoc#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415), [ocaml/odoc#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416), [ocaml/odoc#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417))
- [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) is developing an LRU-based memory bound for the merlin indexer ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)), under active review from [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) has two OxCaml PRs open in odoc covering kind annotations ([ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)) and unboxed named types ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)), both currently conflicting against master
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) opened a chdir removal PR in opam ([ocaml/opam#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)) expected to also fix the long-standing [ocaml/opam#3171](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3171)

- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) is advancing a four-part cmdliner completion overhaul in opam: source compatibility ([ocaml/opam#6854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6854)), vendored cmdliner 2.1.1 ([ocaml/opam#6867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6867)), completion functions ([ocaml/opam#6868](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6868)), and PowerShell support ([ocaml/opam#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869))
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened a new manual chapter for js_of_ocaml documenting Babel-based older browser targeting ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)), including an end-to-end cram test suite
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) opened a roadmap update for odoc ([ocaml/odoc#1421](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1421))
- [@desumn](https://github.com/desumn) opened a community PR adding a `DUNE_PKG` environment variable to dune ([ocaml/dune#14318](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14318))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **dune ↔ ocamlfind**: [ocaml/dune#13229](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13229) tracks relocatable compiler support in dune; [ocaml/dune#14290](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14290) was opened specifically for relocatable ocamlfind support as a prerequisite
- **ocamlfind ↔ opam**: Windows build misgeneration in ocamlfind on Cygwin is tracked in [ocaml/opam#6732](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6732); the MSYS2/mingw-w64 variant is tracked in [ocaml/opam-repository#28636](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/28636), both referenced by [ocaml/ocamlfind#112](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/112)
- **merlin → ocaml-lsp**: the automated flake lock update in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) picked up merlin commit `b3ee185` (2026-04-22), which corresponds to the end-of-week merlin state including the index pointer feature from [ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)
- **dune ↔ opam**: the dune 3.23.0 release tracker ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) involves opam file promotion via `dune build @opam`, with the diff check in [ocaml/dune#14108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14108) requiring opam metadata to be updated during the release process
- **OxCaml across dune and odoc**: [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) is maintaining OxCaml CI in dune ([ocaml/dune#14248](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14248), [ocaml/dune#14305](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14305)) while [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) filed tracking issues for OxCaml support in odoc ([ocaml/odoc#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414)–[ocaml/odoc#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417)), reflecting a coordinated push across the toolchain for OxCaml compatibility
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **dune 3.23.0 release coordination** ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)): blockers included the Windows Ctrl+C crash ([ocaml/dune#14302](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14302)) fixed in [ocaml/dune#14316](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14316), and OxCaml revdep failures; nightly build failures from opam generation errors were resolved via [ocaml/dune#14270](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14270)
- **dune release automation** ([ocaml/dune#14281](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14281)): discussion on automating the release process, including opam file promotion via `dune build @opam` and the metadata diff check introduced in [ocaml/dune#14108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14108)
- **OCAMLPATH ordering debate** ([ocaml/ocamlfind#106](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/106)): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) raised that giving `site-lib` precedence over the stdlib directory in `OCAMLPATH` could shadow compiler `META` files in typical opam switches; the PR originates from [ocaml/opam-repository#27712](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/27712)
- **Windows NTFS case-sensitivity bug** ([ocaml/opam#6909](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6909)): upgrading `menhir` on a case-insensitive NTFS filesystem silently lowercases `menhirLib`/`menhirSdk` directories during installation, breaking subsequent dune builds; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) confirmed this as a novel bug
- **opam `depexts` variable interpolation gap** ([ocaml/opam#6908](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6908)): `%{var}%` strings in `depexts` package names are never interpolated, causing MSYS2 users to receive literal unresolved strings passed to `pacman`; references related issue [ocaml/opam#5836](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5836)
- **OxCaml kind-annotation formatting** ([ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)): [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) and [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) debated whether to use `@@` vs `mod` for kind modifiers and how to parenthesise compound expressions such as `(bit32 mod portable) & (float64 mod contended)`
- **merlin LRU indexer semantics** ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)): review exchange between [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) and [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) on correct `Duplicate` link handling inside the new LRU-backed write path, including a discussion of existential type constraints requiring `Obj.magic`
- **js_of_ocaml browser compatibility** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215)): [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) reported ES6 class and `TextDecoder` breakage on iOS 16.7.5 / Safari and Firefox ESR; [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) recommended Babel as the practical path and opened [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Lev/libev adoption in dune**: the alarm-clock thread ([ocaml/dune#14329](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14329)) and Async_io layer ([ocaml/dune#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14243)) were both migrated to the Lev event loop this week, progressively replacing bespoke threading in the scheduler
- **OxCaml support across the toolchain**: active work spans dune CI ([ocaml/dune#14248](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14248), [ocaml/dune#14305](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14305)), odoc rendering ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407), [ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410), [ocaml/odoc#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414)–[ocaml/odoc#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417)), reflecting coordinated effort to support OxCaml across the tools group
- **Windows portability**: multiple Windows-specific issues surfaced simultaneously — NTFS case-sensitivity in opam ([ocaml/opam#6909](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6909)), MSYS2 depexts interpolation ([ocaml/opam#6908](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6908)), ocamlfind build misgeneration ([ocaml/ocamlfind#112](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/112)), and dune shutdown fix ([ocaml/dune#14316](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14316))
- **ocaml-index scalability in merlin**: the pointer-based index format ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051)) and proposed LRU cache ([ocaml/merlin#2050](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2050)) both target reducing resource consumption for large codebases
- **Shell and terminal modernization in opam**: a coordinated four-part cmdliner completion series ([ocaml/opam#6854](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6854), [ocaml/opam#6867](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6867), [ocaml/opam#6868](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6868), [ocaml/opam#6869](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6869)) adds PowerShell support, complemented by ongoing Nushell JSON output work ([ocaml/opam#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873))
- **AI agent contributions in dune**: [@copilot-swe-agent](https://github.com/copilot-swe-agent) opened and had merged the Nix/Copilot dev environment setup ([ocaml/dune#14321](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14321)) and is iterating on a libev C warning suppression fix ([ocaml/dune#14328](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14328)), with human reviewer oversight
- **Modern JavaScript feature exposure in js_of_ocaml**: increasing use of ES6 classes, optional chaining, and `TextDecoder` in generated output is raising questions about older browser and alternative runtime (quickjs) compatibility, discussed in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215)

group_summaries.brief_summary: oxcaml/oxcaml saw a mixed-block overhaul, Flambda2 and backend improvements, and 5.4 merge progress; opam and merlin had smaller but steady updates.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Mixed block v6, uniform blocks for all-value records, improved int comparison codegen, Flambda2 switch-table extended, Random.State locality.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **All-value/void mixed records and variants** now compile to uniform blocks, enabling polymorphic comparison and marshalling and removing the 254-field limit; mixed block version bumped to v6 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- **`[@@flatten_floats]` attribute** now required to preserve flattened representation for records mixing `float` and `float64` fields; without it the compiler no longer flattens ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5795](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5795) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- **`caml_modify` skipped for `non_pointer` fields** in mixed blocks, avoiding unnecessary write-barrier overhead ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- **Improved codegen for integer comparisons**: bit-result comparisons use `xor` before comparison instead of zero-extending after, and three-way comparisons use `select (x >= y) (x > y) -1`, reducing chain latency and instruction count ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5738](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5738) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet))
- **Flambda2 switch-table optimization** extended to naked numbers and symbols, covering patterns like variants returning `float32` or `int32` constants ([oxcaml/oxcaml#3242](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3242) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell))
- **`Random.State` functions** annotated as accepting the state `@ local`, allowing stack-allocated random states without heap allocation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5908](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5908) by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic))
- **`Dummy_use` operation removed** from the register allocator backend, simplifying the backend after the operation was found to be ineffective at keeping temporaries live ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5614](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5614) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet))
- **Diamond-aware mode hints** improve error messages when a mode Meet or Join involves parallel lattice elements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5858](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5858) by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges))
- **Syntax coloring added to the fexpr printer** for Flambda2 IR dumps, making debug output easier to read in terminals ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5925](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5925) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp))
- **Syntax documentation for all scannable axis annotations** in Merlin expanded to cover `non_pointer`, `non_pointer64`, `non_float`, `separable`, `maybe_separable`, `non_null`, and `maybe_null`, each with hover text and documentation links ([oxcaml/merlin#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923))
- **`-Ix` flag support in Merlin** so both `-I` and `-Ix` compiler load path flags are correctly parsed, with a new test suite verifying behavior ([oxcaml/merlin#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923))
- **`oxcaml-patch-guards` mechanism** pushed to main in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository), allowing multiple versions of upstream patched packages to coexist in an OxCaml switch ([oxcaml/opam-repository#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27))
- **Updated patched upstream packages** added: `ocamlbuild.0.16.1+ox`, `sedlex.3.7+ox`, `topkg.1.1.1+ox`, `uutf.1.0.4+ox`, `ctypes.0.24.0+ox`, `ctypes-foreign.0.24.0+ox` ([oxcaml/opam-repository#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27), [oxcaml/opam-repository#23](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/23))
- **Documentation: mutable field modalities** now documented in the modes syntax reference ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5897](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5897) by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs))
- **Documentation: `with-kind` syntax rationale** added explaining how `type t with ty @@ modality` behaves for mode-crossing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5896](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5896) by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs))
- **Public docs terminology cleanup**: removed monadic/comonadic language ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5893](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5893)), replaced "upstream compiler" with "stock OCaml" ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5894](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5894)), fixed extension docs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5898](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5898)) — all by [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **[oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**: High-activity week with 20 contributors; major work on mixed-block representation overhaul ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5795](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5795), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831)), Flambda2 optimizations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#3242](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3242), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5886](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5886), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5901](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5901)), backend improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5738](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5738), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5614](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5614)), and continued 5.4 merge progress

group_summaries.activity_summary: 5.4 merge advanced across typing, testsuite, and stdlib; preemptible fibers, copy propagation, and .cmx efficiency PRs newly opened.
group_summaries.activity:
- **5.4 merge progress**: labeled tuples support landed ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5692](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5692) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)), `Iarray` module re-oxidised ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5882](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5882)), effect backtrace improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5924](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5924) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)), type checker fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5859](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5859)), shape mechanism fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5888](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5888)), and multiple testsuite fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5911](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5911), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5915](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5915), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5919](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5919), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5920](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5920), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5926](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5926))
- **Newly opened: Preemptible fibers** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913) by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark)), building on the initial preemption PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881)) to add preemptible-fiber abstractions; the preemption PR received an arm64 port and 256-bit register test validation this week
- **Newly opened: Copy propagation pass** for linscan and greedy allocators ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5914](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5914) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)), reducing moves by ~3-5%, plus a fix making the `-copy-propagation` flag functional ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5930](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5930))
- **Newly opened: `.cmx` export efficiency** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5929](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5929) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) and [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)), avoiding traversal and rewrite of non-exported code
- **Newly opened: Remove `unique_` and `once_` legacy mode prefix syntax** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5931](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5931) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)), converting all test files to modern `@ mode` syntax
- **Newly opened: Speculative inlining fix** for lifted constants ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5917](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5917) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)), preventing the inliner from over-counting gains when code is merely lifted rather than simplified
- **Newly opened: Kind annotation soundness fix** for existential types in patterns ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5935](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5935) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)), fixing a bug where kind annotations on existential type variables could silently narrow their declared kind
- **Newly opened: `float64` records as mixed blocks** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)), converting all-`float64` records from float-array blocks to mixed blocks with `[@@represent_as_float_array]` for legacy compatibility
- **Merged: Effects/continuations correctness** — fixed `caml_continuation_use_noexc` not being called when resuming unhandled continuations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5906](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5906) by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark)), and `epoll_wait` no longer fatal-errors on `EINTR` in the tick thread ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5904](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5904))
- **Merged: Flambda2 correctness fixes** — fixed bug in `meet` for array element kinds ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5771](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5771) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)), fixed `lambda_to_flambda` generating unnecessary `%is_int` switches for GADT matches ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5879](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5879) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp))
- **Merged: `caml_raise_async` bug fix** — was incorrectly calling `caml_raise` instead ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5846](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5846) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes))
- **Ongoing: Layout polymorphism** — `env_alloc_mode to Tpat_fun_layout` merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5662](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5662) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn)); layout polymorphic functions draft ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870) by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr))
- **Ongoing: Metaprogramming** — first-class modules in quotes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5884](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5884) by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski)), basic functorization support draft ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5903](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5903) by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn))
- **opam-repository: dune 3.22.2+ox** in progress as draft PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)), blocked on `odoc` CI failures
- **opam-repository: Bootstrap compiler update** to OCaml 5.4.1 in `ocaml-variants` opam file remains open with a merge conflict ([oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36) by [Eduardo Souza](https://github.com/udesou))
- **janestreet/opam-repository: OxCaml repository pointer update** ([janestreet/opam-repository#75](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository/issues/75) by [Xiaomin Liu](https://github.com/xl4624)) received supportive review from [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) and awaits action
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Merlin ↔ oxcaml/oxcaml**: The Merlin compiler sync ([oxcaml/merlin#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234)) explicitly incorporates changes from `5.2.0minus-37`, resolving conflicts around the `-Ix` include flag introduced in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654); the scannable axis syntax-doc PR ([oxcaml/merlin#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235)) tracks the evolving kind system in oxcaml/oxcaml
- **opam-repository ↔ oxcaml/oxcaml**: The patched upstream packages pushed to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) main ([oxcaml/opam-repository#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27), [oxcaml/opam-repository#23](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/23)) and the `ocaml-variants` bootstrap compiler PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#36](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/36)) are directly downstream of the 5.4 merge effort in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)
- **janestreet/opam-repository ↔ oxcaml/opam-repository**: PR [janestreet/opam-repository#75](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository/issues/75) updates the OxCaml opam-repository pointer in the Jane Street repository, representing a coordination between the two package repositories
- **Shared contributor: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)** drove 5.4 merge progress in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) (labeled tuples [oxcaml/oxcaml#5692](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5692), Iarray [oxcaml/oxcaml#5882](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5882)) and simultaneously pushed the infrastructure changes to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) main
- **Shared contributor: [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)** reviewed and merged PRs in both [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5920](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5920), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5919](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5919)) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) ([oxcaml/merlin#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234), [oxcaml/merlin#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235))
- **`odoc` CI failures** block work in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository): the `dune 3.22.2+ox` PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39)) was opened specifically to resolve `odoc` issues, but encountered the same failures; the closed CI fix PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38)) also targeted this problem
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Preemption arm64 porting** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881)): [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) confirmed the `preemption256` test validates that 256-bit YMM registers are correctly saved across preemptions via `caml_preempt` in gdb; discussion of using `expect_asm` tests to lock down assembly output
- **Kind annotation safety for existential types** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5935](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5935)): detailed discussion between [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) and [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) about conditions under which existential types need checking even when not explicitly named, and a subtle `non_float` error arising from `apply_or_null_r` separability constraints
- **`.cmx` export API design** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5929](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5929)): [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) raised questions about whether the fold-based approach could cause de-duplication issues, suggesting exposing `free_imported_function_slots_and_value_slots` rather than folding all free names
- **Mutable field modalities documentation** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5897](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5897)): discussion between [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) about how explicit modalities interact with the implicit mutable-field modality, leading to a documentation update
- **Data flow and variant unboxing poison values** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5923](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5923)): [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) identified a case where data flow analysis cannot see through alias relationships when poison values are involved, preventing switch simplification
- **`odoc` CI blocker in opam-repository** ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39)): [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) opened the `dune 3.22.2+ox` PR hoping to resolve `odoc` CI failures, but encountered the same failures and switched the PR to draft; [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse)'s earlier CI stabilisation PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38)) was closed without merge
- **janestreet/opam-repository pointer update** ([janestreet/opam-repository#75](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository/issues/75)): [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) expressed support for the change and asked whether the PR should be reopened
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Mixed block representation overhaul** progressing systematically: uniform blocks for all-value mixed records ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774)), `float64`-only records moving to mixed blocks ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5796](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5796)), and `[@@flatten_floats]` as an explicit opt-in ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5795](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5795)) — all from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), pointing toward a more uniform memory representation model
- **Compilation speed investment** visible across multiple concurrent efforts: `imported_names` removal ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5886](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5886)), physical equality fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5901](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5901)), `.cmx` export optimization ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5929](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5929)), initial type provision profiling ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5922](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5922)), and CMX loading profiling ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5910](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5910))
- **Effects and preemption infrastructure** advancing in parallel: correctness fixes to the continuation stack ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5906](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5906)), tick thread robustness ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5904](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5904)), and preemptible fibers as a new abstraction ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5913](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5913))
- **Public documentation terminology standardization**: a batch of PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5893](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5893), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5894](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5894), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5896](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5896), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5897](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5897), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5898](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5898)) from [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) removing internal compiler terminology from user-facing docs
- **Merlin incrementally expanding OxCaml language extension coverage** in its syntax-doc feature, with kind modifiers and scannable axes now surfacing documentation on hover ([oxcaml/merlin#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235)), tracking the evolving kind system in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)
- **`odoc` CI stability** remains an ongoing obstacle in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository), blocking both the `dune 3.22.2+ox` PR ([oxcaml/opam-repository#39](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/39)) and the broader CI stabilisation effort ([oxcaml/opam-repository#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Ephemeron GC fixes, type system regressions, Windows compatibility work, and the primitive aliases RFC advancing in ocaml/RFCs.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Ephemeron list API added to C runtime, reserved_header_bits exposed to ocamltest, and -Wformat-signedness warning enabled.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Ephemeron list API** ([ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)): [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) added higher-level helper functions to `runtime/caml/weak.h` — `caml_ephe_list_tail`, `caml_ephe_list_cons`, `caml_ephe_list_append`, `caml_ephe_list_pop` and variants — refactoring `major_gc.c` to use these abstractions and reducing duplication.
- **`reserved_header_bits` exposed to ocamltest** ([ocaml/ocaml#14744](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14744)): [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) added `val reserved_header_bits : int` to `ocamltest_config.mli`, allowing tests to query how many bits of a block header are reserved, and fixing `atomic-locs/cmm.ml` when ocamlopt is absent.
- **`-Wformat-signedness` C compiler warning** ([ocaml/ocaml#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14694)): [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) enabled the `-Wformat-signedness` warning when available, fixing discrepancies between `%x`-family format specifiers and signed/unsigned argument types across the runtime.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) saw six merged PRs covering ephemeron runtime internals ([ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)), a type checker regression fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14768](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14768)), and C compiler warning coverage ([ocaml/ocaml#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14694)).
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no merged PRs but active discussion continued on the primitive aliases RFC ([ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44)), which carries a `positive-committee-recommendation` label.
- A major new proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775)) implementing the modular IO RFC was opened this week, directly connecting the two repositories.
- Windows compatibility received attention via two active bug reports ([ocaml/ocaml#14758](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14758), [ocaml/ocaml#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14767)) and a fix PR ([ocaml/ocaml#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764)).
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche)'s ongoing ephemeron GC correctness series continues to grow, now spanning seven related PRs.
group_summaries.activity_summary: Multiple PRs opened for Windows fixes, ephemeron GC correctness, type system improvements, and extensible IO channels; primitive aliases RFC discussion ongoing.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) proposing **extensible IO channels**, implementing the [modular IO RFC](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/blob/master/rfcs/modular_io.md) with a polymorphic channel type alongside existing native channels.
- [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774) **Ephemerons: wait on all domains before asking for another marking round**, continuing the multi-PR ephemeron GC correctness series.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14763](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14763) **Lambda: merge nested labelled applications**, addressing a spurious intermediary closure created during partial application of optional-argument functions.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14772](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14772) **typechecker debugging: better unscoped printing**, adding a `U:` prefix printer for unscoped identifiers in debug output.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14770](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14770) proposing removal of the rigid variants mode for unification, and [ocaml/ocaml#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769) adding a regression test for a typechecker loop with conjunctive types.
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14771](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14771) **debugger: breakpoint on tfunctor**, enabling breakpoints on module-dependent functions.
- [Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764) fixing a logic error in `socket_poll_add` in `select_win32.c`.
- [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14761](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14761) fixing a build error on OpenSUSE caused by a conflict between `--with-relative-libdir` and a non-standard `libdir` default.
- [Anarchos](https://github.com/Sylvain78) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14773](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14773) adapting the testsuite for Haiku OS specifics.
- Merged [ocaml/ocaml#14768](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14768) ([Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel)): fix for type checker regression in `nondep_type_rec_aux` ([ocaml/ocaml#14766](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14766)).
- Merged [ocaml/ocaml#14709](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14709) ([Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)): ocamldoc now uses `Module_name (Library name)` page title format.
- Merged [ocaml/ocaml#14755](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14755) ([Yang Lin](https://github.com/y1lan)): corrected a broken link in `testsuite/HACKING.adoc`.
- **Primitive aliases** RFC [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) by [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) remains open with `positive-committee-recommendation`; a clarifying exchange with [Clément Blaudeau](https://github.com/clementblaudeau) narrowed the scope of `with external x = y` constraints.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775), opened by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube), directly implements the [modular IO RFC](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/blob/master/rfcs/modular_io.md) tracked in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs), making it a concrete cross-repository deliverable from an approved RFC.
- The primitive aliases RFC [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) is at the design stage but will eventually require implementation work in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml); the current discussion clarifies constraints to simplify future compiler implementation.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14767) **Win32: opendir returns EUNKNOWNERR -267** — [Adrián Montesinos González](https://github.com/amongonz) reported that `Sys.readdir` fails on Windows with an unmapped error code; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) confirmed the bug and suggested mapping `ERROR_DIRECTORY` to `ENOTDIR`.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14758](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14758) **[Win32] `Unix.select` raises 'original file handle not found'** — [Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) provided a detailed analysis, discovered an additional logic error in `socket_poll_add`, and opened [ocaml/ocaml#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764) with a fix; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) encouraged the patch submission.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14760](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14760) **OCaml 5.5.0~beta1 configure failure on OpenSUSE** — [Török Edwin](https://github.com/edwintorok) traced the issue to `libdir=${exec_prefix}/lib64` triggering a false conflict with `--with-relative-libdir`, and opened [ocaml/ocaml#14761](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14761) with a fix.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14765](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14765) **Sys_error exception in Format.flush_standard_formatters** — [Olivier Andrieu](https://github.com/oandrieu) reported that closing stdout before program exit causes an uncaught `Sys_error` from OCaml's format library finalizers.
- [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) — [Nailen Matschke](https://github.com/nmatschke) clarified that a `with external x = y` constraint form would not allow abstract primitives and that existing `with type t = u` constraints already cover the plausible use cases, effectively closing off a potential scope expansion of the RFC.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Ephemeron GC overhaul**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has a series of related PRs ([ocaml/ocaml#14722](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14722), [ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732), [ocaml/ocaml#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733), [ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734), [ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735), [ocaml/ocaml#14745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14745), [ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774)) systematically fixing ephemeron marking correctness and refactoring the runtime, following bugs exposed by multicore randomized testing.
- **Windows platform fixes**: Multiple issues ([ocaml/ocaml#14758](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14758), [ocaml/ocaml#14767](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14767)) and PRs ([ocaml/ocaml#14764](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14764)) highlight continued effort to improve Win32 compatibility in Unix and I/O subsystems.
- **Type system regression tracking**: The well-foundedness check ([ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648)) continues to surface edge cases ([ocaml/ocaml#14754](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14754), [ocaml/ocaml#14766](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14766), [ocaml/ocaml#14769](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14769)), with reactive fixes from type system contributors.
- **RFC-to-implementation pipeline**: [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) translating the [modular IO RFC](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/blob/master/rfcs/modular_io.md) into a concrete PR demonstrates active follow-through from RFC design to compiler implementation.
- **Primitive aliases RFC** [ocaml/RFCs#44](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/44) is narrowing in scope through committee-supported design discussion, moving toward a more tractable implementation target.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Quiet week across the multicore group; only picos saw activity with a follow-up on OCaml 5.5 support.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Minimal activity across the multicore group during week 17 of 2026.
- Eight of nine repositories ([eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio), [domainslib](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib), [ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring), [meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio), [saturn](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/saturn), [eio-trace](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio-trace), [kcas](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/kcas), [ocaml-iomux](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux)) recorded no new commits, PRs, or issues.
- [picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos) was the only repository with recorded activity, limited to a follow-up comment on a previously merged PR.
group_summaries.activity_summary: A follow-up comment was posted on the OCaml 5.5 compatibility PR in picos.
group_summaries.activity: - [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) posted a follow-up comment on [ocaml-multicore/picos#387](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos/issues/387) ("Add support for OCaml 5.5"), a PR originally opened in February 2026 and merged on 2026-03-17.

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Dune 3.23 ships and targets 3.24; OxCaml mixed-block overhaul and 5.4 merge advance; OCaml 5.5.0 beta1 lands in opam-repository.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: Dune gains native patch and Lev I/O; OxCaml gets uniform mixed blocks, improved int codegen, and Random.State locality; opam-repository adds Valkey Eio client, IDNA library, and ATD 4.2.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Native OCaml patch action** vendors `hannesm/patch` to replace external `patch`/`gpatch` binary dependency, fixing portability issues [ocaml/dune#14177](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14177) ([ocaml/dune#12232](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12232), [ocaml/dune#7699](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/7699))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Async_io ported to Lev** rewrites dune's async I/O layer to use the bundled `libev`-based event loop, with the alarm-clock thread also migrated ([ocaml/dune#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14243), [ocaml/dune#14329](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14329))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Trace file backups** — `trace.csexp` is now backed up as `trace.csexp.old` before each build ([ocaml/dune#14269](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14269))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`dune tools` reference documentation** added covering all subcommands (`exec`, `install`, `which`, `env`) ([ocaml/dune#14198](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14198))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune version bumped to 3.24** as main branch targets the upcoming release after 3.23 preparations ([ocaml/dune#14333](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14333))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Index pointer system for ocaml-index** — merged indexes now store pointers to constituent index files rather than embedding data inline, reducing file sizes; adds `Outdated_store` exception and `is_on_disk` predicate to the `Granular_marshal` API ([ocaml/merlin#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **MenhirLib snapshot refreshed** in Merlin, resolving copy-rule failures with recent menhir versions ([ocaml/merlin#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **GitHub Actions CI for ocamlfind** covering Linux, macOS, and Windows with OCaml 4.14, 5.4, and 3.08 ([ocaml/ocamlfind#113](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind/issues/113))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **All-value/void mixed records and variants** now compile to uniform blocks, enabling polymorphic comparison and marshalling and removing the 254-field limit; mixed block version bumped to v6 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5774](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5774) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`[@@flatten_floats]` attribute** now required to preserve flattened representation for records mixing `float` and `float64` fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5795](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5795) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`caml_modify` skipped for `non_pointer` fields** in mixed blocks, avoiding unnecessary write-barrier overhead ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Improved codegen for integer comparisons** — bit-result comparisons use `xor` before comparison and three-way comparisons use `select (x >= y) (x > y) -1`, reducing chain latency and instruction count ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5738](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5738) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Flambda2 switch-table optimization** extended to naked numbers and symbols, covering patterns like variants returning `float32` or `int32` constants ([oxcaml/oxcaml#3242](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/3242) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`Random.State` functions** annotated as accepting the state `@ local`, allowing stack-allocated random states without heap allocation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5908](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5908) by [Vesa Karvonen](https://github.com/polytypic))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`Dummy_use` operation removed** from the register allocator backend, simplifying the backend after the operation was found to be ineffective ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5614](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5614) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Diamond-aware mode hints** improve error messages when a mode Meet or Join involves parallel lattice elements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5858](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5858) by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Syntax coloring added to the fexpr printer** for Flambda2 IR dumps, making debug output easier to read in terminals ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5925](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5925) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Syntax documentation for all scannable axis annotations** in Merlin expanded to cover `non_pointer`, `non_pointer64`, `non_float`, `separable`, `maybe_separable`, `non_null`, and `maybe_null` with hover text and documentation links ([oxcaml/merlin#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`-Ix` flag support in Merlin** so both `-I` and `-Ix` compiler load path flags are correctly parsed, with a new test suite verifying behavior ([oxcaml/merlin#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`oxcaml-patch-guards` mechanism** pushed to [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) main, allowing multiple versions of upstream patched packages to coexist in an OxCaml switch ([oxcaml/opam-repository#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Ephemeron list API** added to C runtime — `caml_ephe_list_tail`, `caml_ephe_list_cons`, `caml_ephe_list_append`, `caml_ephe_list_pop` and variants refactor `major_gc.c` to use higher-level abstractions ([ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`reserved_header_bits` exposed to ocamltest** — adds `val reserved_header_bits : int` to `ocamltest_config.mli`, allowing tests to query reserved block header bits ([ocaml/ocaml#14744](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14744) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`-Wformat-signedness` C compiler warning** enabled when available, fixing discrepancies between `%x`-family format specifiers and signed/unsigned argument types ([ocaml/ocaml#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14694) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml 5.5.0 beta1** added to opam-repository via [ocaml/opam-repository#29750](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29750) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), with coordinated compatibility fixes for mdx, ppx_import, earlybird, goblint, and more
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **valkey.0.2.0** — Eio-native OCaml 5 client for the Valkey key-value store with RESP3, TLS, cluster routing, and auto-reconnect ([ocaml/opam-repository#29783](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29783) by [Avi Fenesh](https://github.com/avifenesh))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **idna.0.4.0** — Pure-OCaml IDNA2008 hostname validation with Punycode and Unicode NFC normalization, no C dependencies ([ocaml/opam-repository#29801](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29801) by [Yuriy Krasilnikov](https://github.com/YuriyKrasilnikov))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **atd.4.2.0** (13 packages) adds Java list aliases, OCaml YAML output, and jsonlike serializers ([ocaml/opam-repository#29802](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29802) by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **bos.0.3.0** with XDG directory support, realpath, and a deadlock fix ([ocaml/opam-repository#29791](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29791) by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **cmdliner.2.1.1** with bash-completion and Windows path fixes ([ocaml/opam-repository#29788](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29788) by [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **dns.10.2.5** (13 packages) with constant-time TSIG MAC verification security fix ([ocaml/opam-repository#29779](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29779) by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **ocaml-ai-sdk.0.3** adds Anthropic native structured outputs, HTTP timeouts, and new Claude model catalog ([ocaml/opam-repository#29761](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29761))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **conf-mingw-w64 spring-clean** (~70 packages) hardening pkgconf calls and adding consistent metadata as preparatory work for MSYS2 UCRT64 support ([ocaml/opam-repository#29795](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29795) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **wire.0.9.0** — binary wire format DSL with EverParse 3D output ([ocaml/opam-repository#29786](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29786)), **alcobar.0.3.1** — Alcotest-compatible fuzzing library ([ocaml/opam-repository#29784](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29784)), both by [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **ocgtk preview1** — GTK4 bindings for OCaml announced via Planet post ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3624))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **"Tailwind Without Node"** blog post by [Thomas Gazagnaire](https://github.com/samoht) describing pure-OCaml Tailwind CSS v4 with browser-side compilation via js_of_ocaml ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3625))
